programs.program_course_name: Accounting and Finance (Masters Stage) - UK Campus - MSc
programs.academic_requirements: Applicants are normally expected to have one of the following: Any of the six CCAB accounting professional qualifications, which could be any of ACCA, CIPFA, ACIEW, ACAS, ACAI and CIMA final stage examination completion. Equivalent overseas qualifications which have been fully recognised by any of the CCAB members or the University.

minimum_english_language_score_requireme.IELTS: 6.0 overall with no less than 5.5 in each band

programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: Applicants are normally expected to have one of the following: Any of the six CCAB accounting professional qualifications, which could be any of ACCA, CIPFA, ACIEW, ACAS, ACAI and CIMA final stage examination completion. Equivalent overseas qualifications which have been fully recognised by any of the CCAB members or the University.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

essential: A 2:2 honours degree or equivalent in Acting or a related subject.

funding_and_scholarships.name: Graduate Scholarship
funding_and_scholarships.description: BCU graduates enrolling onto an eligible Master’s programme for the first time can receive a 20% fee reduction.

funding_and_scholarships.name: Master’s loans
funding_and_scholarships.description: Government-backed loans of up to £12,858 are available to UK, Irish and eligible EU nationals studying postgraduate Master’s courses in any subject area.

programs.academic_requirements: Applicants will normally be expected to have a 2:2 honours degree, or equivalent, in a related subject area (e.g. Acting, Drama, Musical Theatre, Performing Arts, English and Drama) but potential students with a degree in another discipline or with experience in a related professional area would also be considered. Previous performance training is essential for this course.
programs.additional_information: Entry is by audition. Applicants are required to register their interest for the next Open Day in December.

Previous performance training is essential for this course.. programs.program_course_name: Advancing Diabetes Care MSc - Module / MSc / PgDip
programs.academic_requirements: Applicants must hold current professional registration with a UK regulatory body (NMC, HCPC, GMC). In addition, applicants must normally meet one of the following requirements: Have studied to BSc Hons or equivalent, ideally to include a Research Methods module; Have a minimum of three years in your area of clinical practice and a first degree or evidence of recent professionally related study at diploma or degree level; Have completed degree-level study within the last five years or the Birmingham City University module Essential Academic Skills to be completed prior to starting the programme.
programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: Applicants must hold current professional registration with a UK regulatory body (NMC, HCPC, GMC). In addition, applicants must normally meet one of the following requirements: Have studied to BSc Hons or equivalent, ideally to include a Research Methods module; Have a minimum of three years in your area of clinical practice and a first degree or evidence of recent professionally related study at diploma or degree level; Have completed degree-level study within the last five years or the Birmingham City University module Essential Academic Skills to be completed prior to starting the programme.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

programs.academic_requirements: Applicants are normally expected to have a minimum of a 2:2 honours degree in any discipline or three years of professional work experience in a role which has required you to work and interact with a range of people requiring a regular element of problem-solving. For the Fintech pathway, applicants need a background in finance (e.g., an undergraduate degree in a finance-related subject such as Accountancy, Finance, Economics or Business Management) or three years of professional work experience in a finance role.

programs.academic_requirements: Applicants should have a good pass in a computer-related discipline, at a level equivalent to UK Level 5, with the equivalent of 240 UK credits (120 ECTS credits). For example a Computing HND with an overall merit profile. Examples of courses which could be considered include: BTEC Higher National Diploma (HND), SQA Higher National Diploma, Chinese three year Dazhuan Diploma, Malaysian Advanced Diploma, Nigerian Higher National Diploma, United Arab Emirates Higher College of Technology Higher Diploma, Romanian Diplomă de Absolvire (a Colegiului Universitar) (Higher Education Diploma (Short-cycle), NCC Level 5 Diploma. If you are an EU/international student, please see further information below.
programs.international_qualifications: IELTS 6.0 overall with no less than 5.5 in each band
programs.additional_requirements: If English is not your first language, you will need to provide evidence of your proficiency such as IELTS or TOEFL test scores.

accepted_qualifications.qualification: Pearson BTEC (Diploma / Extended Diploma/ Extended Certificate)
accepted_qualifications.entry_requirements:
112 UCAS Tariff points
Diploma accepted with one A Level or equivalent level 3 qualifications
Extended Certificate accepted with two A Levels or equivalent level 3 qualifications

Pass with 60 credits, 45 credits at level 3
Accepted subjects: Social Sciences, Humanities or Law

additional_information.details: You will require use of a laptop, and most students do prefer to have their own. However, you can borrow a laptop from the university or use one of our shared computer rooms.
You will receive £5 print credit in each year of your course, available after enrolment.

not_included_fees.cost: Once you have spent your £5 credit, additional printing on campus costs from 5p per sheet.

not_included_fees.estimated_cost: Based on the past experience of our students, you might find it helpful to set aside about £30 for each year of your studies for your personal stationery and study materials.

programs.academic_requirements: Accepted qualifications include ABE Advanced Diploma with at least a grade B in two modules and the qualification must be in a relevant subject area; Diploma in Higher Education (DipHE) pass with 120 credits at level 4 and 120 credits at level 5, in a related pathway. Acceptance will be Subject to satisfactory comparability of modular content at level 4 and 5. A transcript will be required; Foundation degree (Level 5): Pass with 120 credits at level 4 and 120 credits at level 5, in a related pathway. A minimum of 60% overall must be achieved. Acceptance will be subject to satisfactory comparability of modular content at level 4 and 5. A transcript will be required; Higher National Diploma (HND): Pass with 120 credits at level 4 and 120 credits at level 5, in a related pathway. A minimum of a Merit profile overall. Acceptance will be subject to satisfactory comparability of modular content at level 4 and 5. A transcript will be required.
programs.guidance_for_international_students: There are three ways to apply: Direct to the University, Through a country representative, or Through UCAS.
programs.financial_support: Further information on possible undergraduate financial support is available from Birmingham City University and the government.
programs.additional_information: Applications are considered on an individual basis for EU/international applicants.

additional_information.video_transcript: View video transcriptSpeaker 1 Hi, I'm Danielle and today we are going to be discussing everything that you need to know about a postgraduate degree. Hi, I'm Izzy and I'm studying Fashion Design. Speaker 2 My name's Josh and I'm studying Sport and Exercise Nutrition. My name's David and I'm studying Physiotherapy. Speaker 1 My name's Danielle and I'm studying dietetics. So what is a postgraduate degree? It's any course that you do after an undergraduate degree. So this could be a Master's or you may go on to do a PhD after your Master's. You can also do a PGCE if you wanted to go into the more teaching route or you can do more specialist courses if you wanted to go on to do architecture or nursing.
